raw_title: Book Review: ‘The Ending Writes Itself,’ by Evelyn Clarke
book_title: ‘The Ending Writes Itself,’ by Evelyn Clarke
description: In this novel, a group of authors race to finish a mystery manuscript, only to find themselves part of a lethal plot.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘American Fantasy,’ by Emma Straub
description: In Emma Straub’s latest novel, “American Fantasy,” a pop group’s midlife return provides fodder for both comedy and redemption on the high seas.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Corto Maltese,’ by Hugo Pratt
description: “Corto Maltese,” Hugo Pratt’s influential 1967 graphic novel, returns, with just as much to say about childhood during wartime.

raw_title: Book Review: ‘Hexes of the Deadwood Forest,’ by Agnieszka Szpila
book_title: ‘Hexes of the Deadwood Forest,’ by Agnieszka Szpila
description: The Polish best seller “Hexes of the Deadwood Forest” is like a post-porn fever dream of Eastern European magic realism crossed with a plant-based “Joy of Sex.”
